synthesis.target_compound: Silver nanoparticles (Pf-AgNPs) synthesized using Perilla frutescens leaf extract

steps.description: Powdered plant leaves (5 g) were heated in 100 mL of distilled water at 70°C for 60 min to prepare the aqueous leaf extract. The cooled and filtered solution was preserved at 4°C for further use.
AgNO3 (2 mM) was dissolved in P. frutescens aqueous leaf extract (2.5% v/v). The mixture was sonicated for 90 min at 60°C in the dark.
The solution was centrifuged at 9000 rpm for 30 min at 25°C.
The sediment obtained was washed three times.

synthesis.notes: The whole reaction was carried out in the dark to prevent photo-activation. Optimum conditions were determined as 2 mM AgNO3 and 2.5% plant extract. The bio-reduction of Ag+ to Ag0 was confirmed by color change from pale yellow to dim brown. The synthesized Pf-AgNPs had a crystallite size of ~15 nm (XRD), hydrodynamic diameter of 28.3–161 nm (DLS, most <61 nm), spherical/oval shape (FESEM), and zeta potential of −18.1 mV.
